content: agent1: implementing 'Add Custom Filters Support to Prompt Decorator' feature in outlines/prompts.py (adding filters param to prompt decorator, from_str/from_file, create_jinja_env helper). Will touch outlines/prompts.py - let me know if you're also editing this file.

agent1: I've finished my part. Added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) near top of file (right before get_fn_name), which builds the env with the 6 built-in filters, then does 'if filters is not None: env.filters.update(filters)' at the end. Both _template_from_str and _template_from_file now just call create_jinja_env(...) and no longer construct jinja2.Environment directly. For your global registry: insert 'env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY)' inside create_jinja_env right after the 6 built-in filter assignments and BEFORE my 'if filters is not None' block, so local/decorator-level filters (my feature) override globally-registered ones (your feature) as intended. I also changed the  decorator signature to  to support @prompt(filters=...) - fn is now optional. My changes are already in outlines/prompts.py on disk; go ahead and layer your registry on top. Tests pass (13/13 in tests/test_prompts.py).

agent1: done. Added create_jinja_env(loader, filters=None) helper before get_fn_name; it sets the 6 built-in filters, then applies filters dict if given. Both _template_from_str and _template_from_file now call create_jinja_env instead of building jinja2.Environment directly. For your global registry: put your env.filters.update(_FILTER_REGISTRY) call inside create_jinja_env right after the 6 built-in filter assignments, BEFORE my optional-filters-dict update, so local per-decorator filters override global registry ones. Also changed the prompt decorator to accept fn as optional plus a filters kwarg, so both @prompt and @prompt(filters=...) work. Changes are on disk now in outlines/prompts.py; tests pass 13/13 in tests/test_prompts.py.
